channel.title: NPR Topics: News
channel.description: NPR news, audio, and podcasts. Coverage of breaking stories, national and world news, politics, business, science, technology, and extended coverage of major national and world events.
channel.copyright: Copyright 2024 NPR - For Personal Use Only

item.title: Did you know? Alan Greenspan and Ayn Rand were close friends
item.description: One of the most intellectually important relationships in the life of the late Fed chair Alan Greenspan was with his close friend, the formidable novelist and libertarian thinker Ayn Rand.
item.content:encoded:
One of the most intellectually important relationships in the life of the late Fed chair Alan Greenspan was with his close friend, the formidable novelist and libertarian thinker Ayn Rand.

(Image credit: David Hume Kennerly)
item.dc:creator: Anastasia Tsioulcas

item.title: 2 students in custody after shooting at high school in Philippines kills 3
item.description: An investigation is underway to determine the cause. Police said the suspects claimed they were bullied at school.
item.content:encoded:
An investigation is underway to determine the cause. Police said the suspects claimed they were bullied at school.

(Image credit: James Daantos)
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press

item.title: Supreme Court allows a ruling that ends a tool to protect minority voters in 7 states
item.description: The Supreme Court has left in place a ruling that strikes down a key tool for enforcing Voting Rights Act protections for voters with a disability or an inability to read or write in seven states.
item.content:encoded:
The Supreme Court has left in place a ruling that strikes down a key tool for enforcing Voting Rights Act protections for voters with a disability or an inability to read or write in seven states.

(Image credit: Jemal Countess)
item.dc:creator: Hansi Lo Wang

item.title: 8 things to know about the gut microbiome and keeping yours healthy
item.description: Wellness influencers often talk about fixing a broken gut microbiome. And marketers sell tests and supplements to fix your gut health. Here's what what the evidence really shows about gut health.
item.content:encoded:
Wellness influencers often talk about fixing a broken gut microbiome. And marketers sell tests and supplements to fix your gut health. Here's what what the evidence really shows about gut health.

(Image credit: diane555)
item.dc:creator: Will Stone

item.title: Alan Greenspan, the legendary former Federal Reserve chair, dies
item.description: During his chairmanship, Greenspan was celebrated as possibly the best central banker in history. But later, his reputation was tarnished by the worst financial crisis since the Great Depression.
item.content:encoded:
During his chairmanship, Greenspan was celebrated as possibly the best central banker in history. But later, his reputation was tarnished by the worst financial crisis since the Great Depression.

(Image credit: Paul J. Richards)
item.dc:creator: Scott Horsley

item.title: UK PM Keir Starmer resigns. And, US and Iran agree to roadmap for final deal
item.description: British Prime Minister Keir Starmer announced his resignation on Monday. And, the U.S. and Iran have agreed to a "roadmap" to reach a final deal within 60 days.
item.content:encoded:
British Prime Minister Keir Starmer announced his resignation on Monday. And, the U.S. and Iran have agreed to a "roadmap" to reach a final deal within 60 days.

(Image credit: Henry Nicholls)
item.dc:creator: Brittney Melton

item.title: Keir Starmer has resigned, paving way for a 7th U.K. prime minister in 10 years
item.description: Prime Minister Keir Starmer is stepping down. His likely successor, Andy Burnham, a popular former mayor, would become the U.K.'s seventh prime minister in 10 years.
item.content:encoded:
Prime Minister Keir Starmer is stepping down. His likely successor, Andy Burnham, a popular former mayor, would become the U.K.'s seventh prime minister in 10 years.

(Image credit: Wiktor Szymanowicz)
item.dc:creator: Lauren Frayer

item.title: Voters weigh what kind of Democrat they want for Utah's new, blue congressional seat
item.description: The reliably Republican state has its first blue-leaning seat and Utah Democrats are excited for the pickup opportunity. First, they just have to make it through the competitive primary.
item.content:encoded:
The reliably Republican state has its first blue-leaning seat and Utah Democrats are excited for the pickup opportunity. First, they just have to make it through the competitive primary.

(Image credit: Hannah Schoenbaum)
item.dc:creator: Saige Miller

item.title: Despite state bans, abortions have almost doubled. The reason? Pills via telehealth
item.description: States that have banned abortion are suing to stop mailing of abortion pills over state lines. But the telehealth providers say no matter the outcome, they can adapt, and so will their patients.
item.content:encoded:
States that have banned abortion are suing to stop mailing of abortion pills over state lines. But the telehealth providers say no matter the outcome, they can adapt, and so will their patients.

(Image credit: Charles Krupa)
item.dc:creator: Kate Wells

item.title: AI and tech are trying to influence the midterm elections
item.description: The massive spending and heated rhetoric in midterm races reflect the AI industry's political fault lines and competing visions of what the future should look like.
item.content:encoded:
The massive spending and heated rhetoric in midterm races reflect the AI industry's political fault lines and competing visions of what the future should look like.

(Image credit: Natalie Behring)
item.dc:creator: Shannon Bond
